The '''''Megador''''' was a [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] in the service of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] during the [[Swarm War]], when it served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] and then later at the [[Battle of Uroro Station]] during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="EGW" />

==Characteristics==
''Megador'' had an array of sixteen [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]]. It also appears that ''Megador''{{'s}} hangar complement consisted of at least fifty-one hangar bays. The vessel had an observation deck complete with a dome, where commanders could observe their surroundings during fleet maneuvers.<ref name="The Swarm War" />

During the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], it was equipped with three [[long-range turbolaser]] batteries, which had a total firepower that eclipsed the total firepower of five [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]].<ref name="Invincible" />

==History==
The Star Dreadnought ''Megador'' was originally in the service of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Blitzer Harrsk]], whose [[Zero Command|forces]] were located in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In the aftermath of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]]'s [[Imperial Reunification|unification]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]], the ''Megador'' was left abandoned at [[Deep 3]], one of Harrsk's [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], with the Star Dreadnought [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']]. In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], during his survey of Imperial armories in the Deep Core, [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] stumbled upon Deep 3 and procured both Star Dreadnoughts for the Imperial Remnant's war against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Confident that the New Republic did not know of the existence of the ''Megador'' or the ''Dominion'', Pellaeon sought to use the [[Warship/Legends|warships]] to his advantage. The ''Megador'' eventually saw action in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] at the [[Battle of Anx Minor]], a defeat that proved costly for the Remnant, where it was damaged.<ref name="EGW" />

The Super Star Destroyer's service continued for decades. When [[Sien Sovv]] was killed early into the second year of the [[Dark Nest Crisis]], Galactic Alliance [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Cal Omas]] asked Pellaeon to assume the role of Supreme Commander of the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force]].<ref name="The Unseen Queen">''[[Dark Nest II: The Unseen Queen]]''</ref> When the Swarm War broke out a few months later, Pellaeon took command of the Defense Force from aboard the ''Megador'', making it his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]].

Early into the war, ''Megador'' participated in the [[Battle of Nickel One]] in the [[Roche asteroid field]], where it arrived in time to assist [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] in their successful attempt to stop a [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] coup of the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] hive. At [[Sarm]], ''Megador'' was laden with a wing of Jedi [[StealthX]]s and assisted in the [[Battle of Sarm|assault of the planet]] in an attempt to capture or kill [[Lomi Plo]].<ref name="The Swarm War" /> With Lomi Plo's escape apparent, ''Megador'' was moved to coordinate the [[Battle of Tenupe|attack]] the planet [[Tenupe]] in order to assist the Luke Skywalker in his hunt of Lomi Plo, which resulted in an assault against the captured [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Admiral Ackbar (Victory-class)|''Admiral Ackbar'']], which led to the capture of [[Raynar Thul|UnuThul]] and the death of Lomi Plo.

At the conclusion of the war, ''Megador'' would be the site of the Galactic Alliance treaty negotiations with the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], as well as the Luke Skywalker's declaration that the Jedi order would cease to be an entity within the Galactic Alliance government by way of the [[Galactic Alliance Advisory Council|Advisory Council]]. It was there that Skywalker revealed his intentions to form a Jedi council to oversee matters concerning the administration of the [[New Jedi Order]].<ref name="The Swarm War" />

The ''Megador'' was still in service as of [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] and was planned to have a key role in the [[Battle of Kuat (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Kuat]], serving as a refueling and rearming point for an entire wing of [[StealthX]] fighters under the command of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="Inferno" />

It later traveled to the [[Transitory Mists]] along with the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']] and a fleet of [[Imperial Remnant]] ships. There, it participated in the [[Battle of Uroro Station]].<ref name="Invincible" />

In [[44 ABY]], three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the battle for [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], capital of the Galactic Alliance, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] launched off ''Megador'' to go to the ''[[Dragon Queen II]]'', flagship of [[Queen Mother]] [[Tenel Ka Djo]]. A [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of [[Void Jumper]]s led by Admiral [[Gavin Darklighter]] stood in formation to see the ''Millennium Falcon'' off. Admiral [[Nek Bwua'tu]] gave departure clearance himself.<ref name="Apoc">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Apocalypse]]''</ref>
